title: Rokha rig template (skeleton) v1
description: The machine-readable workflow skeleton a creator ships next to a skill (assets/rig.json) and publishes as a rig listing's metadata.rig. It is what makes the rig adoptable and runnable on Rokha: the Builder's template picker instantiates it, adopt-on-run converts it into the runner's own executable rig, and the rig product page reads its input declaration. THE EXECUTED CONTRACT IS `steps[].instruction` plus the pinned skill's SKILL.md — a step's `command` is informational documentation for agents reading the raw skeleton, never executed verbatim. All strings are truncated server-side to the limits noted here; the skeleton is treated as untrusted published content.

name.description: The rig's display name. Its lowercased-dashed form is the rig's page slug (rokha.ai/@handle/<slug>).

intent.description: One sentence: what the whole workflow produces.

intent_category.description: Optional grouping hint (e.g. audit, monitor, explore).

input.description: The rig's declared run-time input — the page's input field labels itself from this, and the run's input flows into step 1.

kind.description: text = free text; skill = the input names a registry skill/tool (the Explore/Audit template pattern). Rendered as a text field either way.

steps.description: The workflow chain, executed in order (v1 adopts as a linear chain). A step WITH a pin runs that skill; a step WITHOUT a pin is a pure instruction (agent) step.

role.description: Informational: 'primary' for the load-bearing tool step, 'step' otherwise.

query.description: Registry search query the Builder uses to (re)resolve this step against the live registry when no pin is honored.

pin.description: Pin a SPECIFIC published skill — used verbatim instead of resolving `query`. NOTE the key is `source` (the registry source key, e.g. 'rokha'), not 'provider'.

instruction.description: THE EXECUTED CONTRACT: what this step actually does. For a scripted skill, spell out the real invocation and flags here (the sandbox agent composes the command from this plus the pinned skill's SKILL.md). Include your knob conventions (e.g. the `dashboard` param: on = also pass --app-html ./rokha-app.html; off = data-only).

expects.description: What flows IN (the rig input, or the previous step's output).

params.description: Editable run knobs seeded into the adopted copy (scalars only; string values ≤200 chars). Convention: `dashboard: "on"|"off"` controls whether the run ships ./rokha-app.html.

command.description: INFORMATIONAL ONLY — the canonical invocation, as documentation for agents reading the raw skeleton. It is NEVER executed verbatim by Rokha; keep the real recipe in `instruction` + the skill's SKILL.md, and keep this line consistent with them.
